description: This PPG should be read in conjunction with APS 220 Credit Risk Management.
Under Attachment C of APS 220, ADIs must apply a buffer over a loan's interest rate of at least 3.0 per cent, unless determined otherwise by APRA.
APRA expects an ADI to have appropriate processes to determine whether loans approved through overrides would be treated as standard or non-standard loans for capital purposes under APS 112.
An ADI is required to have regard to the requirements governing security valuation practices, including valuation of security in the form of property, as detailed in Attachment A of APS 220.
Defaulted exposure is defined as a non-performing exposure as defined in APS 220 Credit Risk Management.
Defaulted property exposures must be risk-weighted in accordance with Attachment E to this Prudential Standard rather than Attachment A.
The loan amount for LVR purposes includes any undrawn committed amount as set out in Attachment C to this Prudential Standard.
Property valuations must be appraised independently using prudently conservative criteria consistent with the requirements set out in APS 220.
Unsettled and failed transactions must be risk-weighted in accordance with Attachment D to APS 112 rather than Attachment B.
Defaulted exposures must be risk-weighted in accordance with Attachment E to APS 112 rather than Attachment B.
Where Attachment B requires risk weights based on external credit ratings, ADIs must also comply with the requirements in Attachment F to APS 112.
Residential and commercial property exposures in covered bond cover pools must meet the standard loan requirements in paragraphs 3 to 7 of Attachment A to APS 112.
Unrated or non-qualifying covered bonds must be treated as bank exposures and risk-weighted using Table 7 of Attachment B.
Exposures through a third party as detailed in paragraph 41 must not be included as corporate exposures.
Rated specialised lending exposures with issue-specific external credit ratings must use Table 10 risk weights.
After applying CCFs, ADIs must risk-weight off-balance sheet exposures according to the requirements in Attachments A, B and D to APS 112.
Where a rated entity has guaranteed another entity's exposure, the guarantee may be recognised for risk-weighting purposes if the criteria in Attachment I are satisfied.
Cash-funded credit-linked notes issued by the ADI against banking book exposures may be treated as cash collateralised transactions if they fulfil the criteria for credit derivatives in Attachment J.
Paragraph 33 requires that SFTs covered by an eligible bilateral netting agreement must meet the requirements detailed in Attachment H to this Prudential Standard.
Paragraphs 6 and 8 of Attachment H reference Attachment G for eligible collateral criteria and the simple or comprehensive approach for calculating RWA when netting loans and deposits.
Paragraph 10 of Attachment I requires that the currency mismatch haircut for guarantees be adjusted in accordance with paragraph 32 of Attachment G regarding revaluation frequency.
Paragraph 3 of Attachment J states that qualifying cash-funded credit linked notes may be treated as cash-collateralised transactions under Attachment G.
Paragraph 17 of Attachment J requires that the currency mismatch haircut for credit derivatives be adjusted in accordance with paragraph 32 of Attachment G regarding revaluation frequency.
Paragraph 46 refers to Attachment A for detailed requirements on collateral valuation policies and processes.
Paragraphs 108-109 refer to Attachment B for the prescribed provisioning approach that APRA may permit or require an ADI to implement.
Attachment B references APS 112 for the definition of eligible lenders mortgage insurer and for eligible collateral provisions applicable to cash on deposit deductions.
